preview_text:
The model assesses Argentina as a decisive favorite with a 90% win probability, leaving Cape Verde with virtually no chance of victory on a neutral pitch. The underdog is credited only with a 10% draw probability under the statistical forecast.

The outcome distribution reflects a highly asymmetric match. Argentina's win probability dominates so completely that competitive scenarios—draws or upsets—are compressed into the remaining 10% of outcomes. The model sees little realistic path to Cape Verde avoiding defeat.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on Cape Verde's prospects. The sportsbook consensus assigns the underdog a 5% win probability and 12% draw chance, totaling 17% non-loss probability compared to the model's 10%. Polymarket prices lie between the two, crediting Cape Verde with 3.7% win odds. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment better reflects the true competitive balance.
